summary: Completed testing of PDF download, print, and fpdf2 PDF generation fixes. All 16 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI verified - Download PDF button triggers file download via fetch+blob approach, Print button opens PDF in new window. PDF generation using fpdf2 is working correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: PDF endpoint returns Content-Type: application/pdf
Backend: Downloaded PDF has valid %PDF-1.3 header (fpdf2)
Backend: PDF has Content-Disposition: attachment header with filename
Backend: PDF has reasonable size (775KB for test invoice)
Backend: server.py has no ReportLab imports
Backend: server.py has no WeasyPrint imports
Backend: PDF is valid and complete with proper structure
Frontend: Download PDF button has data-testid
Frontend: Invoice view page loads with all action buttons visible
Frontend: Download PDF button triggers actual file download (not new tab)
Frontend: Print button opens PDF in new window
critical_code_review_comments: PDF generation using fpdf2 is working correctly - generates valid PDF files with proper structure
WeasyPrint has been completely removed from the codebase
handleDownloadPDF correctly uses fetch+blob+createObjectURL+anchor click approach for direct download
handlePrint opens PDF URL in new window and triggers print() on load
All action buttons (Edit, Mark as Paid, Download PDF, Print, Delete) are visible and functional

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Download PDF and Print buttons verified working)

Key implementations: 1) fpdf2-based PDF generation in /app/backend/utils/pdf_generator.py (pure Python, zero system deps), 2) /api/invoices/{id}/pdf endpoint returns valid PDF with Content-Disposition: attachment header, 3) handleDownloadPDF in InvoiceView.jsx uses fetch+blob+createObjectURL+anchor click for direct download, 4) handlePrint opens PDF URL in new window and calls print() on load. WeasyPrint has been completely removed.
